Skills and slash commands

Source: vignettes/skills-usage.Rmd
skills-usage.Rmd

What are skills?

Skills are reusable prompt templates that the agent can invoke on demand. Type /skillname in the chat to activate a skill, or the agent will auto-trigger matching skills based on your request.

Built-in skills

codeagent ships with skills for common R package development tasks:

Skill Description
/plan Enter read-only planning mode
/verify Run tests and check the package
/compact Compact the context window
/roxygen Generate roxygen2 documentation
/testthat Write unit tests
/document Run devtools::document()
/lint Style and lint R code
/news Update NEWS.md
/explore Explore a data.frame

Using skills from the chat 

# In the Shiny app or REPL:
/plan add a new summarise_by_group() function
/roxygen summarise_by_group
/testthat summarise_by_group

Creating custom skills

Skills are name/SKILL.md directories in any of the search paths:

~/.codeagent/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
.codeagent/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md    # project-local
.btw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
.claude/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md

SKILL.md format:

---
name: my-skill
description: Short description shown in skill picker
argument-hint: "<what to type after /my-skill>"
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Bash
---

Skill body — instructions for the agent.

Use $ARGUMENTS to insert the user-supplied arguments.

Skill discovery 

# List all available skills
list_skills_meta()

# Load a skill prompt programmatically
load_skill_prompt("plan", args = "add feature X", cwd = getwd())

On this page
